sourceMovie.title: Child 44
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Child 44 (2015) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Daniel Espinosa's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Primal Fear
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Child 44 clicked for you, Primal Fear (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Child 44?
faq.answer: Try Primal Fear, The Devil All the Time, The Night of the Hunter—each shares DNA with Child 44 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Child 44?
faq.answer: Child 44 (2015) is best described as Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Child 44 worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
